reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 1 (Dead Code): PASSED - validateDataColumn is actively used as gossip validator (subscriber.go:316). Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): PASSED - Attack path is via P2P gossip (untrusted entry point). Gate 3 (Code Verification): PASSED - Code reading is factually correct. validate_data_column.go:157 checks hasSeenDataColumnIndex during validation, returns ValidationAccept at L226. The subscriber handler (subscriber_data_column_sidecar.go:42) calls setSeenDataColumnIndex AFTER validation completes and handle is invoked (subscriber.go:397). This is a classic TOCTOU race. Gate 4 (Exploitability): PASSED - Attacker can trigger by broadcasting identical data column sidecars to different peers simultaneously; both pass the cache check concurrently before either marks the tuple as seen. Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference): 01e entry PROP-6a4369e9-inv-012 (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:280-297) states 'Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance' with assertion 'forall s1,s2: accepted(s1) && accepted(s2) && same_dedup_key(s1,s2) => s1 == s2'. The code violates this invariant due to the race window. Gate 6 (Scope Check): PASSED - In-scope PeerDAS gossip validation issue. Severity: Medium severity is appropriate per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json - duplicate acceptance could affect gossip propagation and resource consumption but does not directly cause slashing, network splits, or ETH theft. An attacker can trigger this via P2P by broadcasting two identical data column sidecars to different peers, causing both to accept before either is marked as seen, violating the deduplication invariant.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Deduplication key (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index) must prevent duplicate acceptance.
